And until those courts can be rem

Facts Kept From People.


Fol. five years last past the
Washington supreme court has
been an object of suspicion. For
two years last past It has been the
center of a cyclone of stories of
scandal and corruption. Whether
there is much or little truth in
these stories the people of the
state have not been permitted Of
ficially to determine. Time after
time these efforts have been fus-
trated.. Time after time the long
arm OF some mighty unseen power
has appeared to stretch, out of the
a6arkness and suppress. the inves-
tigation.

the court resigned under the accu-
sation of having accepted corpora
ton money. 1t was afterwards dis
closed that an opinion handed
down by him had been written by
an attorney for the Great Northern
railroad. Within a year a former
chief justice was prosecuted for
embezzlement but the alleged vic
tim of his populations refused to
appear against him. 1t is even
claimed that the money he was
charged with having embezzled
was used to corrupt the court.


And yet the law that these men
wrote, while they were on the
bench, Is still the law Of Washing
ton, even to the opinion written by
the Great Northern attorney. Lit
tie of it has been reversed, none
Of it openly repudiated.


And we are told that this court
can do no wrong.


1t has been charged that In one
case $4,000 and in another case
$4,000 were paid for decisions by
the supreme court in favor of a
powerful public service corpora
ton. 1t has been charged that a
series Of decisions were rendered,
for mysterious reasons, in favor of
another powerful corporate inter-
est, decisions which were without
sound basis in either law or equity.
In another case q man claiming to
have pull with the court offered
litigant favorable decision for
$4,000, the decision being after
wards delivered. 1t is charged
that two men were elevated to the
bench for the express purpose of
reversing an established principle
of law, reversal designed to en-
nance the profits of great en-
ploying corporations.


The Great Difficulty.


The fundamental difficulty lies
in the fact that the court Is not
readily responsive to the will of
the people. II the people would
control their judges the people
must themselves select those
judges. II the people would make
their control 1Psir judges COM
pIete the peol must possess
themselves of the power to remove
their judges. This Is fundamental.
